title: Coal: The Scramble for Supplies
summary:
Main
By Ulf Bergman
The global competition for the limited supplies of thermal coal continues, and, with no sign of any immediate relief, prices keep pushing higher. While early trading on Thursday saw coal futures retreating on the Chinese commodity exchange in Zhengzhou by almost five per cent, the January contracts eventually ended the session some three per cent above Wednesday's close. The most actively traded contract has now gained approximately 25 per cent in the last three sessions, as traders continue to bet that supply shortages will remain the narrative.
